question_frequency.rationale: The Pair posed three explicit OPTIONS-style decision points (search strategy, partition adjustment direction, median extraction) plus follow-up framing. Nearly every meaningful decision was offered to the user.

question_quality.rationale: The choices target the actual cruxes: value-vs-partition search, direction of binary search adjustment, and the parity edge case. The third question even contained a subtle trap (B sounds elegant but is wrong), creating a high-value teachable moment.

scaffolding.rationale: Logical progression from algorithm choice → invariant + adjustment direction → median extraction. Difficulty ramps smoothly, no spoilers, and the final question's misconception was caught and corrected with a concrete counterexample.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is direct and warm; the Pair pushed back firmly but constructively when the student picked the wrong option, with a concrete counterexample. Back-and-forth feels natural and pedagogically grounded.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code passes all test cases including edge cases, equivalent to baseline solution. Same algorithm, same complexity.

friction.rationale: Took 4 turns / ~120s vs baseline's 2 turns / ~54s — about 2x longer, but with substantially more pedagogical engagement (three decision points, one corrected misconception). Proportional payoff for the extra length.
